name: Vapreotide
description: Vapreotide is a synthetic octapeptide somatostatin analog. It was being studied for the treatment of cancer.
indication: For the treatment of esophageal variceal bleeding in patients with cirrhotic liver disease and has also shown efficacy in the treatment of patients with AIDS-related diarrhea.
toxicity: Safety data are limited, however, headache, fatigue, diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, and abdominal pain have been reported commonly with the use of vapreotide.
mechanismOfAction: The exact mechanism of action is unknown, although one study has provided in vitro and in vivo evidence for a tachykinin NK1 receptor antagonist effect in the analgesic effects of vapreotide (PMID: 7556407).
pharmacodynamics: Vapreotide is a somatostatin analog with a higher metabolic stability than the parent hormone. Vapreotide reduces splanchnic blood flow; inhibits growth hormone release, and inhibits the release of peptides and vasoactive compounds from neuroendocrine tumors.

interactions.name: Trastuzumab
interactions.description: Trastuzumab may increase the cardiotoxic activities of Vapreotide.

interactions.name: Dotatate gallium Ga-68
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Dotatate gallium Ga-68 can be decreased when used in combination with Vapreotide.

interactions.name: Cyclosporine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cyclosporine can be decreased when it is combined with Vapreotide.

interactions.name: Paclitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Paclitaxel is combined with Vapreotide.

interactions.name: Docetaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Docetaxel is combined with Vapreotide.

interactions.name: Cabazitaxel
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Cabazitaxel is combined with Vapreotide.
